title: Intricately Smoked Vietnamese Quinoa Bun Rieu
description: A floral and crispy Vietnamese dish featuring quinoa and smoked flavors, perfect for a brunch mood, and friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences, served in a claypot in the form of bun rieu.
instructions: Rinse the quinoa in a fine mesh strainer and drain well.
In a medium saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil, then add the quinoa, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 minutes.
While the quinoa cooks, heat the sesame oil in a small skillet over medium heat and add the diced smoked tofu, cooking until crispy and golden brown.
In a large claypot, combine the cooked quinoa, smoked tofu, rehydrated shiitake mushrooms, rice vinegar, soy sauce, and chopped scallions.
Pour in the claypot broth and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in the crispy fried shallots and pickled carrots.
Serve the bun rieu hot, garnished with additional scallions and crispy fried shallots if desired.
